docs.title: path_tool
docs.text:
Functions:

  • make_dir –

    Create a directory with a backup option.

  • copy_file –

    Copy a file/folder from the source path to the destination path.

  • list_paths –

    List all files/folders in given directories and their subdirectories that match the given patterns.

  • collect_files –

    Collect files from a list of paths (files/folders). Will search files in folders and their subdirectories.

  • scan_paths –

    Check if the paths contains and not contains some files.

  • remove_files_in_paths –

    Remove files in the files list in the paths list.

docs.title: make_dir(path: str, backup: bool = True)
docs.text: Create a directory with a backup option.

docs.title: copy_file(src_path: str, dest_path: str)
docs.text: Copy a file/folder from the source path to the destination path.

docs.title: list_paths(paths: list[str], patterns: list[str], recursive=True) -> list[str]
docs.text: List all files/folders in given directories and their subdirectories that match the given patterns.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: paths : list[str] The list of paths to search files/folders. patterns : list[str] The list of patterns to apply to the files. Each filter can be a file extension or a pattern.

docs.title: Returns:
docs.text: List[str]: A list of matching paths.

docs.title: Example:
docs.text:
folders = ["path1", "path2", "path3"]
patterns = ["*.ext1", "*.ext2", "something*.ext3", "*folder/"]
files = list_files_in_dirs(folders, patterns)

docs.title: Note:
docs.text:
• glob() does not list hidden files by default. To include hidden files, use glob(".*", recursive=True).
  • When use recursive=True, must include ** in the pattern to search subdirectories.
    • glob("*", recursive=True) will search all FILES & FOLDERS in the CURRENT directory.
    • glob("*/", recursive=True) will search all FOLDERS in the current CURRENT directory.
    • glob("**", recursive=True) will search all FILES & FOLDERS in the CURRENT & SUB subdirectories.
    • glob("**/", recursive=True) will search all FOLDERS in the current CURRENT & SUB subdirectories.
    • "/*" is equivalent to "".
    • "/*/" is equivalent to "/".
  • IMPORTANT: "/" will replicate the behavior of "**", then give unexpected results.

docs.title: collect_files(paths: list[str], patterns: list[str]) -> list[str]
docs.text: Collect files from a list of paths (files/folders). Will search files in folders and their subdirectories.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: paths : list[str] The list of paths to collect files from. patterns : list[str] The list of patterns to apply to the files. Each filter can be a file extension or a pattern.

docs.title: Returns:
docs.text: List[str]: A list of paths matching files.

docs.title: scan_paths(paths: list[str], with_files: list[str], without_files: list[str] = []) -> list[str]
docs.text:
Check if the paths contains and not contains some files.

Parameters:

  • paths (list[str]) –

    The paths of dirs to scan.

  • with_files (list[str]) –

    The files that should exist in the path.

  • without_files (list[str], default: [] ) –

    The files that should not exist in the work_path. Defaults to [].

Returns:

  • list[str] –

    list[str]: The paths that meet the conditions.

docs.title: remove_files_in_paths(files: list, paths: list) -> None
docs.text: Remove files in the files list in the paths list.
